extraction.one_line_summary: GALL Felix attacked with DEWULF Stan's help on the final climb, distanced the favorites, won by 1:03 and took yellow by 2 seconds over SKJELMOSE Mattias.
tactical_patterns: SCHÄR Michael led out GALL Felix's attack from the bunch, showing AG2R's willingness to sacrifice breakaway riders for GC ambitions.
DEWULF Stan paced GALL from the early break on the final climb, proving breakaway cooperation can still benefit GC threats.
BARDET Romain attacked multiple times in final kilometer without watching behind, allowing EVENEPOEL and SKJELMOSE to secure bonus seconds unchallenged.
Trek-Segafredo controlled at 2 minutes despite MÄDER Gino being just 2 minutes down, revealing miscalculation of stage winner threat to GC.

rider_signals.GALL Felix: positive: converted multiple second places into first win after AG2R teammate led him out, showing improved finishing after Villars runner-up
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: went backwards on first half of final climb when others were still in yellow jersey group, early weakness signal
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: dropped several times but recovered repeatedly, fighting spirit compensated for inferior legs to salvage second and 6 bonus seconds
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: positive: most aggressive form in quite a while with repeated attacks, but poor tactical awareness in final kilometer gifted bonus seconds
rider_signals.SKJELMOSE Mattias: positive: finished third for 4 bonus seconds and sits 2 seconds off yellow, tactically aware to let BARDET animate while saving podium
rider_signals.DEWULF Stan: positive: sacrificed own result from breakaway to pace GC teammate, indicating AG2R's clear leadership structure
rider_signals.MÄDER Gino: neutral: bridged to break despite being 2 minutes down on GC, Trek misjudged his threat and allowed winning margin
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: neutral: attacked from favorites group but faded to fifth at 1:12, lacks explosive punch on steep gradients
